# checks.structure.StructureCheck { #pycmdcheck.checks.structure.StructureCheck }

```python
checks.structure.StructureCheck()
```

Check that package has a valid directory structure.

Validates that the package follows one of the standard Python
package layouts:

- **src layout**: Package code in ``src/<package_name>/``
- **flat layout**: Package code directly in project root

The check verifies that package directories contain ``__init__.py``
files and are properly structured.

## Attributes {.doc-section .doc-section-attributes}

<code>[**name**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} []{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   The check identifier ("structure").

<code>[**description**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} []{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Human-readable description of this check.

## Examples {.doc-section .doc-section-examples}

Run the structure check:

```python
>>> from pathlib import Path
>>> check = StructureCheck()
>>> result = check.run(Path("."), {"enabled": True})
>>> result.name
'structure'
```

Check a package with src layout:

```python
>>> # For a package with src/mypackage/__init__.py
>>> # result.status == CheckStatus.OK
>>> # result.details contains "Using src layout"
```

## Methods

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [run](#pycmdcheck.checks.structure.StructureCheck.run) | Check package directory structure. |

### run { #pycmdcheck.checks.structure.StructureCheck.run }

```python
checks.structure.StructureCheck.run(package_path, config)
```

Check package directory structure.

Determines the layout type (src or flat) and validates that
the package directories are properly formed with ``__init__.py``
files.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**package_path**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[Path](`pathlib.Path`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Path to the package directory to check.

<code>[**config**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)\[[str](`str`), [Any](`typing.Any`)\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Configuration dictionary for this check. Currently no check-specific options are used.

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[CheckResult](`pycmdcheck.results.CheckResult`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   A CheckResult with:

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[CheckResult](`pycmdcheck.results.CheckResult`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   - OK status if valid src or flat layout found

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[CheckResult](`pycmdcheck.results.CheckResult`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   - WARNING status if packages missing ``__init__.py``

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[CheckResult](`pycmdcheck.results.CheckResult`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   - ERROR status if no package or module found
